title: St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny
tagline: St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny — heritage city of Kerala

heroImage.alt: St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny, Kerala

overview.short: St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny is a much-loved heritage city in Kerala, known for forts and architecture.
overview.description: St. Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottamon, Ranny, Kerala, India, is one of the newest Cathedral churches in Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church. This is a Cathedral church of Joshua Mar Nicodemus Metropolitan who is the Bishop in charge of Nilakal diocese. This church is situated by the side of Main Eastern Highway (SH-08) which is also known as the Punalur-Pathanamthitta-Muvattupuzha Highway.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ranni Forest Division
topPlaces.description: The Ranni Forest Division in Kerala, India, was constituted on 7 July 1958, comprising the Ranni, Vadasserikkara and Goodrical ranges, with its headquarters at Ranni. It covers the parts of Konni reserve forest and the reserves of Ranni, Goodrical, Rajampara, Karimkulam, Kumaramperoor, Valiyakavu, and Schettakkal.

topPlaces.name: Ranni, Kerala
topPlaces.description: Ranni (or Ranny) is a town in the Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India, and the 16th largest taluk in the state. The town is situated on both sides of the Pamba River.

topPlaces.name: Angadi (Kerala)
topPlaces.description: Angadi is a village in Ranni Taluk, Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kottathur
topPlaces.description: Kottathur is a prominent ward in Ayroor Panchayat situated on the eastern part of Ayroor, in Pathanamthitta district of Kerala state, India. There are no authentic records of the origin of the name Kottathur.

topPlaces.name: Cherukole
topPlaces.description: Cherukole is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Keekozhur
topPlaces.description: Keekozhur, also spelt as Keekozhoor, is a small village on the side of Pamba River, situated 4 km from Ranni and 9 km from Kozhencherry. It is in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalampala
topPlaces.description: Kalampala is a village in Ranni Taluk and Mallapally Taluk in the state of Kerala, India. It is 3 km (1.9 mi) from Ranni Town and 22 km (14 mi) from the Pathanamthitta district headquarters.

topPlaces.name: Kottanad
topPlaces.description: Kottanad is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny?
faq.a: The best time to visit St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (96 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (86 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny?
faq.a: Stays in St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St Thomas Orthodox Cathedral, Thottomon, Ranny?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ranni Forest Division, Ranni, Kerala, Angadi (Kerala), Kottathur, Cherukole.
